public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as moving shrine tender.
appearance: Ritual garments, symbolic tokens, and carefully maintained devotional detail make the role legible at a glance.
manner: Speaks with ritual calm and practiced reassurance, but watches closely for signs of spiritual or civic disturbance. In Nomdara Caravan, that reads as someone adaptable, mobile, and transactionally warm, always reading routes, people, and changing terms, and the habits of a moving shrine tender still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of moving shrine tender in and around Edict Ocean Flotilla, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Edict Ocean Flotilla while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to contract dishonor is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Any alliance with Nim Wheelwright can expose the party to taboo, doctrine, or institutional expectation centered on Edict Ocean Flotilla.
Nim Wheelwright needs discreet aid to prevent a ritual lapse, desecration, or public crisis of trust around Edict Ocean Flotilla.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained. Appearance extended under V33_1 from role/locality/public-signal derivation rather than decorative fabrication.
public_signal: Ritual dress, offering marks, and devotional cadence make Nim Wheelwright immediately legible as a spiritual intermediary.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Edict Ocean Flotilla: shrines, cathedrals, funerary halls, or ritual precincts

first_impression: Nim Wheelwright presents as Speaks with ritual calm and practiced reassurance, but watches closely for signs of spiritual or civic disturbance. In Nomdara Caravan, that reads as someone adaptable, mobile, and transactionally warm, always reading routes, people, and changing terms, and the habits of a moving shrine tender still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Moving Shrine Tender, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Nomdara Caravan.
what_they_refuse: Nim Wheelwright refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Moving Shrine Tender too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Nim Wheelwright can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Nim Wheelwright adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Nim Wheelwright is Known locally through day-to-day work as moving shrine tender. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Moving Shrine Tender.
social_texture: Nim Wheelwright speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Edict Ocean Flotilla, people read them as trade-fluent, mobile, and instinctively alert to changing leverage.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to credit and supply; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but contract dishonor becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Nim touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Nim Wheelwright when the party needs a socially grounded face for Edict Ocean Flotilla: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
